  as it's a dependency for the
  ToSourceString method.

╭───────────────────────────────────╮
│ ✓  ReadFolder r8_go/src/utils     │
│                                   │
│ Listed 68 item(s).                │
╰───────────────────────────────────╯

  utils.DescriptorToJavaType is used in
  dex_string.go, so I'll examine
  r8_go/src/utils/descriptor_utils.go to
  confirm its implementation.

│ ✓  ReadFile r8_go/src/utils/desc… │
│                                   │

  utils.DescriptorToJavaType is correctly
  implemented and used. I'll now refactor
  r8_go/src/dex/dex_string.go by
  splitting it into separate files for
  DexString, DexType, DexProto,
  DexMethod, DexField, and
  DexItemFactory. I'll begin by
  extracting DexType into its own file,
  ensuring that all related methods and
  dependencies are correctly handled
  within the dex package.
╭───────────────────────────────────╮
│ ✓  WriteFile Writing to r8_go/sr… │
│                                   │
│ ... first 257 lines hidden ...    │
│ 258         panic("AddSuffix only │
│     valid for class types")       │
│ 259     }                         │
│ 260     d :=                      │
│     t.ToDescriptorString()        │
│ 261     newDescriptor :=          │
│     d[:len(d)-1] + suffix + ";"   │
│ 262     return                    │
│     factory.CreateType(factory.Cr │
│     eateString(newDescriptor))    │
│ 263 }                             │
│ 264                               │
│ 265 func (t *DexType)             │
│     AddSuffixId(index int,        │
│     factory *DexItemFactory)      │
│     *DexType {                    │
│ 266     if index == 0 {           │
│ 267         return t              │
│ 268     }                         │
│ 269     if index < 0 {            │
│ 270         panic("index must be  │
│     >= 0")                        │
│ 271     }                         │
│ 272     return                    │
│     t.AddSuffix(fmt.Sprintf("$%d" │
│     , index), factory)            │
│ 273 }                             │
│ 274                               │
│ 275 func (t *DexType)             │
│     WithSimpleName(newSimpleName  │
│     string, factory               │
│     *DexItemFactory) *DexType {   │
│ 276     if !t.IsClassType() {     │
│ 277         panic("WithSimpleName │
│     only valid for class types")  │
│ 278     }                         │
│ 279     d :=                      │
│     t.ToDescriptorString()        │
│ 280     lastSlash :=              │
│     strings.LastIndexByte(d, '/') │
│ 281     if lastSlash == -1 {      │
│ 282         return                │
│     factory.CreateType(factory.Cr │
│     eateString("L" +              │
│     newSimpleName + ";"))         │
│ 283     }                         │
│ 284     return                    │
│     factory.CreateType(factory.Cr │
│     eateString(d[:lastSlash+1] +  │
│     newSimpleName + ";"))         │
│ 285 }                             │
│ 286                               │
│ 287 func (t *DexType) GetName()   │
│     string {                      │
│ 288     switch                    │
│     t.descriptor.String() {       │
│ 289     case "Z":                 │
│ 290         return "boolean"      │
│ 291     case "B":                 │
│ 292         return "byte"         │
│ 293     case "S":                 │
│ 294         return "short"        │
│ 295     case "C":                 │
│ 296         return "char"         │
│ 297     case "I":                 │
│ 298         return "int"          │
│ 299     case "J":                 │
│ 300         return "long"         │
│ 301     case "F":                 │
│ 302         return "float"        │
│ 303     case "D":                 │
│ 304         return "double"       │
│ 305     case "V":                 │
│ 306         return "void"         │
│ 307     default:                  │
│ 308         d :=                  │
│     t.descriptor.String()         │
│ 309         // Lpkg/Name; -> Name │
│ 310         if len(d) >= 2 &&     │
│     d[0] == 'L' && d[len(d)-1] == │
│     ';' {                         │
│ 311             lastSlash :=      │
│     strings.LastIndexByte(d, '/') │
│ 312             if lastSlash < 0  │
│     {                             │
│ 313                 return d[1 :  │
│     len(d)-1]                     │
│ 314             }                 │
│ 315             return            │
╰───────────────────────────────────╯
